data.current_description: By passing in the appropriate variables this method updates the user's profile
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing user's profile information within the authenticated user's master account. Accepts 13 parameters for modifying user properties such as name, email, preferences, and account settings. Supports partial updates - only provided fields will be modified. Returns the updated user object with all current properties. Side effects: Changes to user settings may affect their access permissions or system behavior.

data.current_description: By passing in the appropriate variables this method creates a new user and assigns it to the master account of the authenticated user. By default this user will have chores and allowance access.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new child user account and automatically assigns it to the authenticated user's master account. Requires 5 parameters including user credentials and profile information. Returns the newly created user object with all account properties. Default permissions: New child users automatically receive access to chores and allowance features. The child user is linked to the master account hierarchy and inherits applicable account policies.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and appropriate. Description lacks specificity about the 5 parameters and what the returned user object contains. Enhanced description clarifies input requirements, specifies the return value structure, and provides more detail about default permissions and account hierarchy relationships for AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'kkid_userlist_delete' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is passive and lacks clarity about permanence. Description lacks context about what happens to related data and why the restriction exists. Enhanced summary emphasizes the permanent nature of deletion. Improved description clarifies the single parameter requirement, specifies the response type, and details important side effects (data loss, access revocation, cascading effects) critical for AI agents to understand the operation's impact.

data.current_description: By passing in the appropriate variables this method deletes the specified user. (This function is restricted to Superusers ONLY)
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a specified user account from the system. Restricted to Superusers only. Requires 1 parameter (user ID or identifier). Returns a 202 Accepted response confirming deletion. Side effects: Deleted user loses all system access; associated chores, allowances, and wishlist data may be archived or removed depending on system configuration; child users linked to deleted master accounts require reassignment.
data.reasoning: Summary is passive and lacks clarity about permanence. Description lacks context about what happens to related data and why the restriction exists.
